title: pseudolachnostylis maprouneifolia
intro: Pseudolachnostylis maprouneifolia, also known as the forest lemon, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pseudolachnostylis maprouneifolia are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antioxidant. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and stems are used in herbal preparations, often combined with other plants for enhanced medicinal effects.
